other_observers: Brian Gibbons, Steve Brown, Brandon Percival, Lisa Edwards, Brad Steger, Mark Peterson, Maggie Boswell.; No; Brian Gibbons and Steve Brown banded the bird.
specific_location: The RMBO banding station at Chico Basin Ranch.
description: The bird was a quite large, chunky warbler that was about 6 inches long. The bird had a dark gray hood, which extended down onto the upper breast, and a very conspicuous, complete white eye ring. The whole upper part of the bird was a uniform light greeny brown, and the whole breast, flanks and the under tail was a bright yellow. The bill was quite large for a warbler and the lower mandible was light. The legs were a bright flesh color. The length of the under tail coverts was not noted.Behaviors: The bird often stayed quite still for several minutes. It did appear to forage occasionally, and flew around to different branches of the Russian Olive 5-6 times. One time after it flew to another thicker branch, it then walked along the branch for 3-4 feet.Call: None heard.Plumage: Alternate
